subHeadline: The verdicts people actually want before they read a single spec sheet — the calls we'd make for a friend, backed by real UK enquiry and failure data.

rankings.why: The modern successor to the OM651 — AdBlue system aside, materially fewer failure reports. 520 enquiries in 2025, weighted toward routine maintenance, not catastrophic failure [MB-VERIFIED].
OM654-only diesel range in the newest, highest-value C-Class generation — a clean ✅ Safe verdict with no caveat in the data.
Post-facelift electronics, the more dependable diesel, and values that have already absorbed the steepest depreciation. £3,000–£5,000 recon against £8,000–£22,000 vehicle value [MB-QUOTE].
The high-performance biturbo V8's full replacement cost dwarfs every other Mercedes engine — £8,000–£14,000 recon, £12,000–£18,000 rebuilt [MB-QUOTE].
Typical values of £28,000–£55,000 against £3,000–£5,000 recon cost — comfortably safe regardless of engine variant.
Ageing host values (£1,500–£5,000) against £2,800–£4,800 recon cost — squarely inside the 70% Mercedes threshold.
7-seat capability, OM642/M276 reliability that's held up well, and large-SUV values that comfortably absorb replacement cost.
The last E-Class before Mercedes' more complex modern electronics — mechanically straightforward, well-understood, and cheaply parted.

dataSources.title: Quick Reference: Data Sources Used in Sections 1–4
